data.description: Completed Tauri to Electron migration. All 18 Rust backend commands ported to TypeScript IPC handlers. Frontend bridge rewritten. Build and dev server verified working. Zero Tauri imports remain in src/.
data.what_changed: Full migration from Tauri v2 to Electron 40 with electron-vite. Rust backend (~770 lines) ported to TypeScript (~500 lines across 4 IPC handler files). Frontend bridge (tauri-commands.ts) rewritten to use window.electronAPI.invoke(). File watching moved from notify-debouncer-mini to chokidar. Claude streaming moved from Tauri Channel to IPC send/on pattern.
data.files_created: electron/main.ts, electron/preload.ts, electron/ipc-handlers.ts, electron/ipc/commands.ts, electron/ipc/claude.ts, electron/ipc/watcher.ts, electron/ipc/settings.ts, electron.vite.config.ts, tsconfig.node.json
data.files_modified: src/lib/tauri-commands.ts, src/hooks/useFileWatcher.ts, src/hooks/useGitStatus.ts, src/components/ControlPanel/ProjectList.tsx, package.json, biome.json, .gitignore, index.html

data.description: Migrated Pane from Tauri v2 to Electron 40 due to WKWebView 60fps cap breaking ProMotion 120Hz support
data.why: WKWebView caps at 60fps on macOS, no workaround, Tauri closed as 'not planned'. Pane needs 120Hz ProMotion to feel like a native IDE. Electron uses Chromium which supports ProMotion natively.
data.alternatives_considered: napi-rs native modules (too complex for I/O-bound code), Rust sidecar (unnecessary overhead), staying with Tauri (dealbreaker)
data.approach: Full TypeScript port — Rust backend was only ~770 lines of I/O-bound code with no performance-critical paths

data.description: Fixed preload script not loading — Electron 40 defaults sandbox:true which blocks contextBridge in preload. Set sandbox:false in webPreferences.
data.symptom: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'on') — window.electronAPI was undefined
data.root_cause: Electron 40 defaults sandbox:true for renderer processes, which prevents preload scripts from using Node.js APIs like contextBridge
data.fix: Added sandbox: false to BrowserWindow webPreferences

data.description: electron-vite with ESM (type:module) requires .mjs output filenames. Default vite lib mode outputs .js which electron-vite can't find. Must set rollupOptions.output.entryFileNames to [name].mjs.
data.context: electron-vite dev failed with 'No electron app entry file found: out/main/main.mjs' because build produced main.js

data.description: Pane architecture changed from Tauri+Rust to Electron+TypeScript. Backend: electron/ipc/ (commands.ts, claude.ts, watcher.ts, settings.ts). Bridge: src/lib/tauri-commands.ts rewired to window.electronAPI.invoke(). Build: electron-vite replaces vite+tauri-cli.
data.files_created: electron/main.ts, electron/preload.ts, electron/ipc-handlers.ts, electron/ipc/commands.ts, electron/ipc/claude.ts, electron/ipc/watcher.ts, electron/ipc/settings.ts, electron.vite.config.ts, tsconfig.node.json
data.files_modified: src/lib/tauri-commands.ts, src/hooks/useFileWatcher.ts, src/hooks/useGitStatus.ts, src/components/ControlPanel/ProjectList.tsx, package.json, biome.json, .gitignore, index.html, src/lib/claude-types.ts

data.goal: Research Zustand v5 selector equality semantics for nested Map/Set state patterns

data.description: Deep-dived into Zustand v5 selector equality mechanics from actual source code (v5.0.11). Key findings documented in response to Aslam.
data.why: Need sub-16ms render performance with nested Map/Set state in projects store
data.source: Direct source code reading of zustand/esm/react.mjs, react-dom-client.development.js updateSyncExternalStore, zustand/esm/vanilla/shallow.mjs

data.description: Replaced per-node React components in FileTree with flat list rendering — single component renders all visible rows via useMemo'd flat array. Eliminated recursive component tree mount/unmount overhead.
data.why: One React component per tree node with 7 Zustand subscriptions each was fundamentally slow. 200 nodes = 1400 selector evaluations per store update. Flat list means one component, one subscription, useMemo to recompute visible rows.
data.alternatives: Virtualized list (react-window) — overkill for typical project trees. Canvas rendering — too complex for the benefit.

data.description: Zustand selectors returning new arrays/objects inside useSyncExternalStore cause infinite re-render loops. Must compute derived arrays via useMemo in render body, not inside selector functions. Selector must return stable references (primitives or existing object refs).
data.why: buildFlatRows() in selector returned new array every call → Object.is fails → re-render → selector runs again → new array → infinite loop

data.description: Per-project FileTree instances with display:none toggling — eliminates DOM churn on project switch
data.approach: Same pattern as Workspace.tsx Conversation components — one FileTree per project, toggled via CSS display. FileTree now takes projectId prop instead of reading activeProjectId from store. All callbacks use prop directly.

data.goal: Trace and identify the source of delay/heaviness when switching between projects in the Pane app

data.description: Identified multiple sources of delay when switching between projects in Pane
data.root_cause: 7 distinct cascading side effects fire when activeProjectId changes
data.impact: Poor UX - UI feels slow/laggy during project switch

data.description: Zustand selector granularity is the #1 performance lever in this architecture. The updateProject helper creates new project objects on every mutation, but sub-properties retain their references. Subscribing to the project object re-renders on every mutation. Subscribing to project.expandedDirs only re-renders when dirs change. This distinction is invisible to developers but huge for render performance — during streaming, FileTree was re-rendering 10-20x/sec for no reason.
